title: The Cave That Creates Its Own Weather System
hook_text: A cave with its own clouds
script: A cloud forms inside a mountain — every single day, on schedule. Waitomo Caves in New Zealand hosts a wind system so consistent it generates its own internal fog banks each morning. The cave exhales warm humid air, meets cooler rock, and produces real clouds — underground. Discovered in 1887, it took geologists decades to explain it. Today, tourists walk through actual weather happening inside solid earth. Would you feel more amazed or terrified standing inside a cloud underground?
search_summary: Waitomo Caves New Zealand generates its own internal weather system including underground fog and clouds daily.
comment_question: Would a cloud underground feel magical or suffocating to you? 🌫️
